agent_scopes.mechanism: project (.factory/droids/) overrides personal (~/.factory/droids/) on name collision — reverse of skills precedence

definition_format.mechanism: .md files in .factory/droids/ (project) or ~/.factory/droids/ (personal); single-file with system prompt, model preference, and tooling policy

invocation_patterns.mechanism: exposed as subagent_type targets for the Task tool; droids enabled by default with live progress reporting in UI

model_selection.mechanism: per-droid model preference field independent of global model setting; enables mixed-model agent compositions

per_agent_mcp.mechanism: no per-droid MCP configuration documented; MCP is workspace-wide

subagent_spawning.mechanism: droids exposed as subagent_type targets for Task tool delegation; /import-subagent converts Claude Code .claude/agents/*.md to droid format

tool_restrictions.mechanism: categorical tool policy using named categories (filesystem, shell, search, browser, web_fetch); differs from per-tool allowlists used by other providers

providerExtensions.name: Custom Droid Format
providerExtensions.description: Agents (Custom Droids) are .md files stored under .factory/droids/ (project) or ~/.factory/droids/ (personal). Each droid defines its own system prompt, model preference, and tooling policy in a single file. The CLI scans top-level files only (no subdirectory recursion).

providerExtensions.name: Project-Over-Personal Precedence
providerExtensions.description: Project-scope droids override personal droids with the same slug. This is the opposite of skills precedence (where personal overrides project) and must be accounted for in any cross-provider conversion.

providerExtensions.name: Tool Categories
providerExtensions.description: Droids specify allowed tools via named categories: filesystem, shell, search, browser, web_fetch, and others. This categorical tooling policy differs from per-tool allowlists used by some other providers.

providerExtensions.name: Per-Droid Model Selection
providerExtensions.description: Each droid can specify its own model preference independently of the global model setting, enabling mixed-model agent compositions within a single project.

providerExtensions.name: Claude Code Subagent Import
providerExtensions.description: The /import-subagent command reads .claude/agents/*.md files and converts them to Factory droid format, providing a migration path from Claude Code agent definitions to Factory droids.
